edges.description: Deficient lysosomal NEU1 blocks processing of sialylated glycoproteins and oligosaccharides, producing sialylated storage metabolites.
Mild but catalytically impaired NEU1 variants produce the residual neuraminidase deficiency underlying the type 1 phenotype.
Nervous-system lysosomal storage is associated with glial LAMP1 accumulation, astrogliosis, and the progressive neurologic syndrome.
intermediate_mechanisms: Lysosomal LAMP1 accumulation and glial activation disrupt neural circuitry and motor function.

edges.description: Sialyloligosaccharide accumulation in perifoveal ganglion-cell and retinal nerve-fiber layers produces the characteristic retinal pathology.
Systemic sialylated metabolite storage is reflected by increased urinary sialylated oligosaccharide excretion.
Impaired lysosomal sialylated substrate catabolism produces urinary O-linked sialopeptide accumulation.
Accumulated abnormal metabolic products in ocular tissues contribute to punctate cataracts.
intermediate_mechanisms: Metabolic-product accumulation in the lens alters lens transparency.

edges.description: Ocular metabolic-product accumulation can manifest as corneal clouding.
intermediate_mechanisms: Storage-related abnormal metabolic products affect transparent ocular tissues.

edges.description: Progressive neuronal storage and glial pathology cause network dysfunction that manifests as seizures.
intermediate_mechanisms: Lysosomal neuronal dysfunction and gliosis lower seizure threshold in cortical networks.

edges.description: Neural storage-related cortical hyperexcitability manifests as progressive myoclonic seizures.
intermediate_mechanisms: Storage-related cortical circuit dysfunction produces progressive myoclonic epilepsy.

edges.description: Nervous-system storage and gliosis disrupt cerebellar and motor circuitry, causing ataxia.
intermediate_mechanisms: Cerebellar and motor circuit dysfunction impairs coordination.

edges.description: Progressive ataxia and motor dysfunction produce gait impairment.
intermediate_mechanisms: Cerebellar and motor pathway dysfunction impair gait coordination.

edges.description: Peripheral nervous system involvement in type 1 can include decreased nerve conduction velocity.
Progressive cortical and motor network dysfunction produces myoclonus.
intermediate_mechanisms: Storage-related cortical hyperexcitability drives progressive myoclonus.

edges.description: Motor circuit involvement can manifest as intentional tremor.
intermediate_mechanisms: Cerebellar and motor pathway dysfunction produce action tremor.

edges.description: Progressive motor and cerebellar pathway involvement can impair speech articulation.
intermediate_mechanisms: Cerebellar and bulbar motor pathway dysfunction disrupt speech motor control.

edges.description: Central motor pathway involvement can manifest as hyperreflexia.
intermediate_mechanisms: Upper motor pathway dysfunction increases reflex responses.

edges.description: Epileptic network dysfunction can produce diffuse spike-wave EEG abnormalities.
intermediate_mechanisms: Cortical hyperexcitability produces epileptiform spike-wave discharges.

edges.description: Progressive neural storage disease can be accompanied by cerebellar and brain atrophy.
intermediate_mechanisms: Chronic storage-related neurodegeneration causes structural brain volume loss in some patients.

edges.description: Ganglion-cell and retinal nerve fiber layer storage produces retinal OCT abnormalities.
Retinal and visual-pathway storage abnormalities impair visual function.
intermediate_mechanisms: Retinal nerve fiber layer and ganglion-cell pathology disrupt visual pathway signaling.

edges.description: Progressive retinal and visual-pathway involvement causes worsening vision.
intermediate_mechanisms: Ongoing storage pathology in retinal ganglion-cell and nerve-fiber layers disrupts visual function.

edges.description: Visual pathway impairment can manifest with nystagmus.
Perifoveal ganglion-cell and RNFL storage creates the macular cherry-red spot.
edges.target: Cherry red spot of the macula

nodes.description: Sialidosis type 1 generally reflects variant combinations retaining some catalytic activity.
